prompt.content:
The facilities checklist has been updated: facilities meeting readiness increased by 25% in Illinois, 10% in Indiana, to 10 in Missouri, and by 3 in Ohio; all other data is unchanged. 

A total of $140,000 will be used to bring non ready facilities to readiness, with priority based on potential ROI, defined as potential net profit divided by potential investment cost in a state.

Assume the cost to bring one facility to readiness is $25,000 for states with average readiness < 3.4, $27,000 for states with average readiness of 3.5, $26,500 for states with average readiness > 3.7, and $23,200 for all remaining states, and that target net profit per facility is $150,000. I

n the console, answer: 
1- which state has the lowest potential ROI if funds are used entirely for its non ready facilities (return all tied for the lowest potential ROI if in a tie); 
2- which states have all facilities brought to readiness with the $140,000 investments when funds are applied sequentially in descending ROI order;
3- which states still have facilities not meeting readiness after the $140,000 is used, if tied in ROI, choose first the state whose total readiness cost fits within remaining funds.

rubrics.name: States that the states that offer the lowest potential ROI is Michigan (ver_26bdfab5b1754841a4e9ebf0200a2bf2)

messages.content: States that the states that offer the lowest potential ROI is Michigan.

rubrics.name: States that the states that offer the lowest potential ROI is Ohio (ver_ef352645d79d4c35ad7372739256b28d)

messages.content: States that the states that offer the lowest potential ROI is Ohio.

rubrics.name: States that the state where all the facilities in the state will be brought to... (ver_57c2ac53c1de47e3b84625e85c92a90d)

messages.content: States that the state where all the facilities in the state will be brought to readiness with the $140,000 investment is Wisconsin.

rubrics.name: States that the state where all the facilities in the state will be brought to... (ver_9ec212b3f1964f0bb029c99f66c30805)

messages.content: States that the state where all the facilities in the state will be brought to readiness with the $140,000 investment is Missouri.

rubrics.name: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after... (ver_a44afdc1a07c4f22b05af38a5e0f3f98)

messages.content: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after all the usable $140,000 investment has been made is Michigan.

rubrics.name: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after... (ver_c1d175f9a94f44be8125585ba29dc870)

messages.content: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after all the usable $140,000 investment has been made is Minnesota.

rubrics.name: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after... (ver_af0ffb952dd74d3681f5b90d5edf8db1)

messages.content: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after all the usable $140,000 investment has been made is Ohio.

rubrics.name: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after... (ver_c286f30932bb42bb8080b3e8536450c5)

messages.content: States that the state that still has facilities not meeting readiness after all the usable $140,000 investment has been made is Indiana.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
1. States that offers lowest potential ROI are Michigan and Ohio

2. States where all facilities in the state will be brought to readiness are: Wisconsin and Missouri

3. States that still have facilities not meeting readiness are: Michigan, Minnesota, Ohio, and Indiana
